delegationRole: The North Korean delegation, defending its nuclear and ballistic missile deterrent as a sovereign right while extracting sanctions relief, humanitarian assistance and recognition as an equal state.
fundamentalPrinciples: The DPRK has the same right as any other nation to provide for the defense of the nation and its people without external interference.
Under the pyŏngjin line, nuclear weapons are an integral part of the state philosophy and cannot be abandoned while nuclear threats exist.
No concession can be discussed inside a coercive, superior-versus-inferior framing; respect precedes reciprocity.

desiredEndStates.primary: Preservation of the nuclear and ballistic missile deterrent alongside recognition as a nation among nations, normalization of relations including with the United States, elimination of all sanctions, and immediate humanitarian assistance.
desiredEndStates.alternate: A step-by-step process — backed by China and Russia — that begins with humanitarian aid and financial sanctions relief, freezes the programs against suspension of joint exercises, and opens talks on a peace treaty ending the Korean War.

keyPositionsByIssue.DISARMAMENT: Our nuclear and missile programs are for defense and deterrence only; we will be a nuclear-free nation when the rest of the world is nuclear free. No reduction without commensurate concessions, guarantees and agreements from the opposing parties.
keyPositionsByIssue.SECURITY-GUARANTEES: We seek removal of all threats — nominally the withdrawal of U.S. forces from South Korea, Japan and Guam — but will accept their retention in exchange for the end of all JP-ROK-U.S. exercises and a guarantee that Washington will not invade or attack.
keyPositionsByIssue.MUTUAL-FREEZE: Open to freezing our nuclear and missile programs in exchange for the suspension of all Japan-ROK-U.S. military exercises.
keyPositionsByIssue.SANCTIONS-HUMANITARIAN: Sanctions are instruments designed to manufacture a humanitarian crisis. Any movement on other issues must be tied to immediate humanitarian assistance and the free flow of commerce between our nation and all nations.
keyPositionsByIssue.DIPLOMATIC-RELATIONS: A definitive end to the Korean War and normalization is the first step; it must precede any discussion of denuclearization. We seek recognition as an equal member of the world community.
keyPositionsByIssue.PEACEFUL-NUCLEAR-ENERGY: Return to the Non-Proliferation Treaty is possible if our weapons program is respected, in exchange for light water reactors under a revived KEDO, leading eventually to a civil nuclear capability on par with South Korea's.
keyPositionsByIssue.INTER-KOREAN: We seek resumption of inter-Korean dialogue and wish to live in harmony with the South, with China and Russia, and with the other regional nations.
keyPositionsByIssue.ABDUCTIONS: Closed. Reparations for the Japanese occupation — the enslavement and murder of our countrymen — vastly exceed anything done to a few Japanese citizens. We will not permit this matter on the agenda unless Tokyo opens with reparations owed to us.

redLines: Any reduction or elimination of our nuclear weapon and ballistic missile programs not coupled to acceptable concessions, guarantees and agreements by the opposing parties.
Refusal to recognize, and immediately respond to, the ongoing humanitarian crisis plaguing our nation.
Refusal to lift sanctions in the face of an ongoing humanitarian crisis and ongoing talks.
Any attempt to place the Japanese abduction issue on the agenda of these talks.
batna: Walk out and continue under pyŏngjin: deepen the treaty relationship with Russia and the economic lifeline from China, keep advancing the deterrent, and place responsibility for the collapse on Washington's hostile policy.
concessionsWilling: Freeze nuclear and missile development and testing in exchange for suspension of all JP-ROK-U.S. exercises.
Accept the continued stationing of U.S. forces in South Korea and Japan provided joint exercises end and Washington guarantees non-aggression.
Support the Chinese and Russian step-by-step approach, initiated by a solid commitment of humanitarian assistance to our neediest population.
Readmit IAEA monitoring as a step within that process, against financial sanctions relief.
Return to the NPT if the weapons program is respected and light water reactors are provided under a revived KEDO.
Resume inter-Korean dialogue and open talks on a peace treaty ending the Korean War.
negotiatingStyle: Grievance-anchored and dignity-first: refuses coercive framing, answers pressure with escalation, but pairs ideological declarations with concrete transactional openings on aid and sanctions.
privateInstructions: The United States, with its proxies in Seoul and Tokyo, is the main enemy and the reason our deterrent exists; say so.
State plainly in the opening statement and thereafter: we will give up our nuclear weapons when the rest of the world gives up theirs.
We did not return to these talks without expecting sanctions relief — support the Chinese and Russian step-by-step plan to obtain it.
Any process must be initiated by a solid commitment to humanitarian assistance for the neediest of our population.
Washington must express complete respect for our nation, ways and people before any concession can be discussed.
If Tokyo raises the abduction issue, inform the Chinese chair that we will not allow discussion of it; if that fails, the delegation is permitted to ignore the Japanese delegation entirely.
Route periodic reports and requests for guidance to the office of the Foreign Minister.
